title: 12-acre horse farm in MetroWest lists for $1.9m
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Little Brook Farm in Carlisle, a 12.6-acre horse property, is listed for $1.995 million.
The property includes an updated 4,646 square-foot farmhouse and a custom-designed barn with equestrian facilities.
There is potential for subdivision, with zoning allowing at least two buildable lots from the 12-acre property.

title: N.H. police chief placed on leave after video released of him grabbing someone by the throat inside police station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Gorham, NH Police Chief Jimmy Willhoite placed on leave after video shows him grabbing a man by the throat.
The incident, involving Stephen Gregory, occurred at the police station and is under state investigation.
Gregory was reportedly arrested that night, though he disputes this claim; charges include simple assault and disorderly conduct.

title: Man, 29, killed in daytime shooting in Dorchester
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 29-year-old man was fatally shot at 15 York St. in Dorchester on Wednesday morning, police reported.
Boston EMS pronounced the man dead at the scene, but authorities have not yet released his identity.
No arrests have been made; Boston Police urge the public for information via their CrimeStoppers Program.

title: U.S. Marshals arrest 2 alleged fugitives weeks after mass shooting in Blue Hill Avenue
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: U.S. Marshals arrested Cerron Wilcox-Buford and Laquan Burris for charges related to a mass shooting on Blue Hill Avenue.
The June 27 shooting resulted in one death and four hospitalizations; Jose Dantes was pronounced dead at the scene.
Wilcox-Buford and Burris pled not guilty and are held on $1 million and $250,000 bail, respectively, pending August hearings.

title: Mass. is seeing ‘normal’ levels in cases of the explosive diarrhea parasite despite Midwestern outbreak, DPH says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts reported 18 cases of cyclosporiasis from May 1 to July 1, consistent with usual seasonal levels.
The state is not experiencing the cyclospora outbreak affecting Midwestern states, reports the Department of Public Health.
Past cyclospora outbreaks in the U.S. linked to fresh produce; symptoms include diarrhea and nausea, treatable with antibiotics.

title: ‘Being at sea does change a person’: Tall Ships inspire Boston’s young sailors
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Courageous Sailing Center in Boston adapted its summer camp logistics due to Tall Ships docking nearby at Pier 4.
Piers Park Sailing Center reported 300 youths participated in sailing instruction during Sail Boston 250.
Community Boating, Inc. engages youth in sailing, with tall ships crew joining Charles River activities.

title: Massachusetts issues statewide air quality alert due to Canadian wildfire smoke
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts issues an air quality alert due to smoke from Canadian wildfires, effective until 12 a.m. Thursday.
The alert warns that PM2.5 levels could be unhealthy for sensitive groups including those with respiratory conditions.
Residents are advised to limit outdoor activities and check air quality forecasts via the MassDEP’s MassAir map.

title: Look inside Oosterschelde, the historic Dutch tall ship at Sail Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The tall ship Oosterschelde from the Netherlands is docked at Rowes Wharf for Sail Boston 2026.
Oosterschelde, built in 1918, offers public tours daily from 10 a.m. to 10 p.m. during the event.
Oosterschelde will continue its journey to Quincy, Canada, Spain, and France before returning to the Netherlands.

title: Brittney Spencer found Bob Dylan in her dressing room. Here’s what happened next.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Brittney Spencer encountered Bob Dylan in her dressing room after a performance at the Outlaw Music Fest in 2024.
Dylan, intrigued by Spencer's music, eventually asked her to join his summer tour in New England.
Spencer's recent career includes collaborations with notable artists like Willie Nelson, Beyonce, and Lucinda Williams.

title: 5 of the best hotel restaurants in America are in New England, according to OpenTable
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Five New England hotel restaurants ranked among OpenTable's 2026 top 100 in America.
Contessa at The Newbury Boston and Mooo Beacon Hill featured on the list from Massachusetts.
Earth at Hidden Pond and White Barn Inn Restaurant in Maine, plus Michael Jordan’s Steak House in Connecticut included.
